trivia_bullets.text: Barge In is from Throne of Eldraine (ELD), released October 4, 2019 — a top-down designed set blending Arthurian legend and Grimm's fairy tales. Giants were one of ELD's committed tribal threads alongside Faeries and Elementals, reflecting the set's fairy-tale-of-the-clumsy-giant archetype.
sources: Wizards.com: 'Eldraine or Shine' by Mark Rosewater, 2019-09-09

trivia_bullets.text: At a single red mana, Barge In is one of the more efficient combat tricks in ELD Limited: +2/+2 to a target attacker AND trample to all attacking non-Human creatures. The 'non-Human' clause directly rewards playing Giants, Wolves, and other ELD tribal creatures. EDHREC rank 12,347 is respectable for a common instant.

"The giant had heard of locks, but he had yet to discover a door he couldn't open." Deadpan fairy-tale humor that mirrors the art exactly — the giant has heard of doors, not read the manual. Captures ELD's comedic register for Giant characters.

Barge In was previewed September 13, 2019 via Limited Resources, a prominent limited-strategy podcast, signaling its relevance to ELD draft. Artist Mark Behm is known for comedic and character-expressive MTG illustrations.

related_cards.name: Rimrock Knight / Boulder Rush
related_cards.note: Adventure card that also gives +2/+0; similar combat-trick role in ELD limited

related_cards.name: Thunderous Snapper
related_cards.note: ELD giant-adjacent creature that rewards non-Human tribal
